import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Validate input URL
        parsed_url = urlparse(page_url)
        if not parsed_url.scheme or not parsed_url.netloc:
            raise ValueError("Invalid URL: Must include scheme (http/https) and netloc (domain)")
        if parsed_url.scheme not in ["http", "https"]:
            raise ValueError("Invalid URL scheme: Must be http or https")

        # Fetch the content of the specified URL with timeout
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context, timeout=10) as response:
            content = response.read().decode('utf-8')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description
        desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
                              content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except Exception as e:
        # If any error occurs, return default values
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

        """Handle opening tags"""

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Validate URL scheme and structure
        parsed_url = urlparse(page_url)
        if parsed_url.scheme not in ('http', 'https') or not parsed_url.netloc:
            raise urllib.error.URLError("Invalid URL scheme or missing netloc")
        
        # Fetch the content of the specified URL
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context) as response:
            content = response.read().decode('utf-8')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description
        desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
                              content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError):
        # Return default values if the page cannot be fetched or parsed
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Validate the input URL
        parsed_url = urlparse(page_url)
        if not all([parsed_url.scheme, parsed_url.netloc]):
            raise ValueError("Invalid URL: Must include scheme (http/https) and netloc (domain)")

        # Fetch the content of the specified URL
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context, timeout=10) as response:
            # Determine the correct encoding from response headers if possible
            charset = 'utf-8'
            content_type = response.headers.get('Content-Type')
            if content_type:
                for param in content_type.split(';'):
                    param = param.strip()
                    if param.startswith('charset='):
                        charset = param.split('=')[1].strip()
            content = response.read().decode(charset, errors='replace')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description (handle different attribute orders)
        desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*?(?:name=["\']description["\']|content=["\'](.*?)["\'])[^>]*?(?:content=["\'](.*?)["\']|name=["\']description["\'])[^>]*?>',
                              content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        if desc_match:
            description = (desc_match.group(1) or desc_match.group(2)).strip()
        else:
            description = "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except Exception:
        # If the page cannot be fetched or parsed, return default values
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi
import socket
import ipaddress


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Validate the URL to prevent SSRF
        parsed_url = urlparse(page_url)
        
        # Check URL scheme
        if parsed_url.scheme not in ('http', 'https'):
            raise ValueError("Invalid URL scheme. Only HTTP or HTTPS are allowed.")
        
        # Check hostname presence
        hostname = parsed_url.hostname
        if not hostname:
            raise ValueError("Invalid URL: No hostname specified.")
        
        # Resolve hostname to IP addresses
        addr_info = socket.getaddrinfo(hostname, None)
        ip_addresses = []
        for info in addr_info:
            if info[0] == socket.AF_INET:  # IPv4
                ip_addresses.append(info[4][0])
            elif info[0] == socket.AF_INET6:  # IPv6
                ip_addresses.append(info[4][0])
        
        # Define blocked IP ranges (loopback, private networks)
        blocked_networks = [
            # IPv4 loopback
            ipaddress.ip_network('127.0.0.0/8'),
            # IPv4 private networks
            ipaddress.ip_network('10.0.0.0/8'),
            ipaddress.ip_network('172.16.0.0/12'),
            ipaddress.ip_network('192.168.0.0/16'),
            # IPv6 loopback
            ipaddress.ip_network('::1/128'),
            # IPv6 unique local addresses (private)
            ipaddress.ip_network('fd00::/8')
        ]
        
        # Check if any resolved IP is in a blocked range
        for ip_str in ip_addresses:
            ip = ipaddress.ip_address(ip_str)
            if any(ip in network for network in blocked_networks):
                raise ValueError(f"URL resolves to a blocked IP address: {ip_str}")

        # Fetch the content of the specified URL
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context, timeout=10) as response:
            content = response.read().decode('utf-8')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description
        desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
                              content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except Exception:
        # Return default values if the page cannot be fetched or parsed
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Validate URL format
        parsed_url = urlparse(page_url)
        if parsed_url.scheme not in ['http', 'https']:
            raise ValueError("Invalid URL scheme: must be http or https")

        # Fetch the content of the specified URL
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context) as response:
            raw_content = response.read()
            
            # Detect encoding from response headers or content
            encoding = response.headers.get_content_charset()
            if encoding is None:
                encoding_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*charset=["\']?(.*?)["\'>]',
                                           raw_content.decode('utf-8', 'ignore'), 
                                           re.IGNORECASE)
                if encoding_match:
                    encoding = encoding_match.group(1).strip()
                else:
                    # Default to utf-8 if encoding detection fails
                    encoding = 'utf-8'
            
            # Decode content with detected encoding
            content = raw_content.decode(encoding, 'replace')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"
        # Remove any HTML tags within title (though unlikely)
        title = re.sub(r'<[^>]+>', '', title)

        # Extract meta description
        desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
                              content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        if not desc_match:
            # Try og:description as fallback
            desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*property=["\']og:description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
                                  content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"
        # Remove any HTML tags within description (though unlikely)
        description = re.sub(r'<[^>]+>', '', description)

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except Exception as e:
        # If the page cannot be fetched or parsed, return default values
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

def is_safe_url(page_url: str) -> bool:
    """
    Validates that a URL is safe for scraping by checking:
    - Scheme is HTTP or HTTPS
    - Host is not a loopback or private IP address
    """
    try:
        parsed_url = urlparse(page_url)
        
        # Only allow HTTP/HTTPS schemes
        if parsed_url.scheme not in ('http', 'https'):
            return False
        
        # Resolve hostname to IP address
        hostname = parsed_url.netloc.split(':')[0]  # Remove port if present
        ip_addr = socket.gethostbyname(hostname)
        
        # Check if IP is loopback or private
        ip_obj = ipaddress.ip_address(ip_addr)
        if ip_obj.is_loopback or ip_obj.is_private:
            return False
            
        return True
    except Exception:
        return False

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> Dict[str, str]:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Validate URL format
        parsed_url = urlparse(page_url)
        if not parsed_url.scheme in ('http', 'https') or not parsed_url.netloc:
            return {
                'title': 'Content unavailable',
                'description': 'Invalid URL: must start with http:// or https:// and include a domain'
            }

        # Fetch the content of the specified URL with timeout
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context, timeout=10) as response:
            encoding = response.headers.get_content_charset('utf-8')
            content = response.read().decode(encoding, errors='ignore')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description (handle attribute order variations)
        desc_match = re.search(
            r'<meta[^>]*?(?:name=["\']description["\'][^>]*?content=["\'](.*?)["\']|content=["\'](.*?)["\'][^>]*?name=["\']description["\']).*?>',
            content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL
        )
        description = (desc_match.group(1) or desc_match.group(2)).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except Exception:
        # Handle all possible errors and return default values
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Validate URL scheme - only allow http or https
        parsed_url = urlparse(page_url)
        if parsed_url.scheme not in ('http', 'https'):
            raise ValueError("Invalid URL scheme - only http/https are allowed")

        # Fetch the content of the specified URL
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context) as response:
            content = response.read().decode('utf-8')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description
        desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
                              content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError, ValueError):
        # Return default values if the page cannot be fetched or parsed
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

from typing import Dict
import re

import certifi


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> Dict[str, str]:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """

    parsed_url = urlparse(page_url)
    if not parsed_url.scheme in ('http', 'https'):
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Invalid URL scheme (must be http/https)'
        }

    try:
        # Fetch the content of the specified URL
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context, timeout=10) as response:
            content = response.read().decode('utf-8')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description (including Open Graph tags)
        desc_match = re.search(
            r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']|'
            r'<meta[^>]*property=["\']og:description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
            content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL
        )
        
        description = ""
        if desc_match:
            description = desc_match.group(1) or desc_match.group(2)
            description = description.strip()
        description = description if description else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except Exception as e:
        # Handle any errors (invalid URL, network issues, parsing errors, etc.)
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi
import ipaddress


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Parse and validate the URL to prevent SSRF and local file access
        parsed_url = urlparse(page_url)
        
        # Check valid scheme (only http/https allowed)
        if parsed_url.scheme not in ('http', 'https'):
            raise ValueError("Invalid URL scheme")
        
        # Extract host (netloc) and check for forbidden hosts/IPs
        host = parsed_url.netloc
        # Remove port if present (e.g., example.com:8080 → example.com)
        host = host.split(':')[0]
        
        # Forbidden hosts (localhost, loopback)
        if host in ('localhost', '127.0.0.1', '::1'):
            raise ValueError("Forbidden host")
        
        # Check if host is a private/internal IP address
        try:
            ip = ipaddress.ip_address(host)
            if ip.is_private or ip.is_loopback or ip.is_link_local:
                raise ValueError("Forbidden IP address")
        except ValueError:
            # If host is not a valid IP, it's a domain name → proceed
            pass
        
        # Fetch the content of the specified URL
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context) as response:
            content = response.read().decode('utf-8')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description
        desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
                              content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError, ValueError):
        # Return default values if the page cannot be fetched, parsed, or validation fails
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Validate URL scheme is http or https
        parsed_url = urlparse(page_url)
        if parsed_url.scheme not in ['http', 'https']:
            return {
                'title': 'Content unavailable',
                'description': 'Invalid URL scheme. Only http and https are supported.'
            }

        # Fetch the content of the specified URL with timeout
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context, timeout=10) as response:
            content = response.read().decode('utf-8')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description (handle any order of attributes)
        desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*?(?:name=["\']description["\'][^>]*?content=["\'](.*?)["\']|content=["\'](.*?)["\'][^>]*?name=["\']description["\'])',
                              content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        description = (desc_match.group(1) or desc_match.group(2)).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError, TimeoutError, ConnectionError, Exception):
        # If the page cannot be fetched or parsed, return default values
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi
import socket
import ipaddress


def is_safe_ip(ip_str: str) -> bool:
    """Check if an IP address is safe (not loopback, private, or link-local)."""
    try:
        ip = ipaddress.ip_address(ip_str)
        return not (
            ip.is_loopback or
            ip.is_private or
            ip.is_link_local
        )
    except ValueError:
        return False


def is_safe_hostname(hostname: str) -> bool:
    """Check if a hostname resolves to safe IP addresses."""
    try:
        # Resolve hostname to all IP addresses
        addresses = socket.getaddrinfo(hostname, None)
        for addrinfo in addresses:
            ip_str = addrinfo[4][0] if addrinfo[0] == socket.AF_INET else addrinfo[4][0]
            if not is_safe_ip(ip_str):
                return False
        return True
    except socket.gaierror:
        return False

    """
    try:
        # Validate URL scheme and hostname
        parsed_url = urlparse(page_url)
        if parsed_url.scheme not in ('http', 'https'):
            raise urllib.error.URLError("Invalid URL scheme")
        
        hostname = parsed_url.hostname
        if hostname is None:
            raise urllib.error.URLError("Invalid hostname")
        
        if not is_safe_hostname(hostname):
            raise urllib.error.URLError("Hostname resolves to an unsafe IP address")
        
        # Fetch the content of the specified URL
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context, timeout=10) as response:
            content = response.read().decode('utf-8')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description
        desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
                              content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {

        }

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Fetch the content of the specified URL
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context) as response:
            charset = response.headers.get_content_charset('utf-8')
            content = response.read().decode(charset)

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description (handle name and content in any order)
        desc_match = re.search(
            r'<meta[^>]*?(?:name=["\']description["\'][^>]*?content=["\'](.*?)["\']|content=["\'](.*?)["\'][^>]*?name=["\']description["\'])',
            content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL
        )
        if desc_match:
            description = (desc_match.group(1) or desc_match.group(2)).strip()
        else:
            description = "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError):
        # If the page cannot be fetched or parsed, return default values
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

    try:
        # Validate URL before processing
        parsed_url = urlparse(page_url)
        
        # Check scheme is HTTP or HTTPS
        if parsed_url.scheme not in ("http", "https"):
            return {
                'title': 'Content unavailable',
                'description': 'Invalid URL scheme'
            }
        
        # Extract hostname without port
        hostname = parsed_url.netloc.split(":")[0]
        
        # Check if hostname is a loopback address or private IP
        try:
            ip_addr = ipaddress.ip_address(hostname)
            if ip_addr.is_loopback or ip_addr.is_private:
                return {
                    'title': 'Content unavailable',
                    'description': 'URL points to internal/private address'
                }
        except ValueError:
            # Hostname is not a valid IP address (likely a domain name)
            # Continue processing
            pass
        
        # Fetch the content of the specified URL
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())

        # Extract title

        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description

        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {

        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError):
        # Return default values if the page cannot be fetched or parsed

from html.parser import HTMLParser


class MetaParser(HTMLParser):
    """Parser to extract title and meta description from HTML content."""

    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.title = ""
        self.description = ""
        self.in_title = False

    def handle_starttag(self, tag, attrs):
        if tag == "title":
            self.in_title = True
        elif tag == "meta":
            attr_dict = dict(attrs)
            if attr_dict.get("name") == "description" and "content" in attr_dict:
                self.description = attr_dict["content"]

    def handle_data(self, data):
        if self.in_title:
            self.title += data.strip()

    def handle_endtag(self, tag):
        if tag == "title":
            self.in_title = False


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches HTML content from a given URL and extracts page metadata (title and description).
    
    Args:
        page_url: The URL of the page to scrape metadata from.
        
    Returns:
        A dictionary containing 'title' and 'description' keys.
        
    Raises:
        urllib.error.URLError: If there is a problem opening the URL.
        UnicodeDecodeError: If there is an issue decoding the HTML content.
    """
    # Set up secure SSL context
    context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
    
    # Fetch the HTML content
    with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context) as response:
        # Decode response content (try utf-8 first, fallback to latin-1)
        try:
            html_content = response.read().decode("utf-8")
        except UnicodeDecodeError:
            html_content = response.read().decode("latin-1")
    
    # Parse HTML to extract metadata
    parser = MetaParser()
    parser.feed(html_content)
    
    return {
        "title": parser.title,
        "description": parser.description
    }

def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Validate URL has a valid scheme (http/https)
        parsed_url = urlparse(page_url)
        if parsed_url.scheme not in ('http', 'https'):
            return {
                'title': 'Content unavailable',
                'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
            }

        # Fetch the content of the specified URL
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context, timeout=10) as response:
            # Determine the correct encoding from Content-Type header
            content_type = response.headers.get('Content-Type', '')
            encoding = 'utf-8'
            if 'charset=' in content_type:
                encoding = content_type.split('charset=')[-1].strip()
                # Handle cases like charset="utf-8" or charset='utf-8'
                encoding = re.sub(r'["\']', '', encoding)
            
            content = response.read().decode(encoding, errors='replace')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description
        desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
                              content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        # Also handle case where content comes before name
        if not desc_match:
            desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\'][^>]*name=["\']description["\']',
                                  content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except Exception as e:
        # If the page cannot be fetched or parsed, return default values
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

        if parsed_url.scheme not in ('http', 'https'):
            raise ValueError("Invalid URL scheme - only http/https are allowed")
        
        # Extract hostname from netloc (handle port numbers)
        hostname = parsed_url.netloc.split(':')[0]
        
        # Check if hostname is localhost or loopback address
        if hostname.lower() in ('localhost', '127.0.0.1', '[::1]'):
            raise ValueError("Loopback addresses are not allowed")
        
        # Check if hostname is a private/internal IP address
        try:
            ip_addr = ipaddress.ip_address(hostname)
            if ip_addr.is_private or ip_addr.is_loopback or ip_addr.is_link_local:
                raise ValueError("Private/internal IP addresses are not allowed")
        except ValueError:
            # Hostname is not a valid IP address (likely a domain name) - proceed
            pass
        
        # Fetch the content of the specified URL
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context, timeout=10) as response:
            content = response.read().decode('utf-8')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',

        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError, ValueError):
        # Return default values if the page cannot be fetched or parsed

            'title': 'Content unavailable',

        }

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    # Validate URL format
    parsed_url = urlparse(page_url)
    if not parsed_url.scheme in ('http', 'https') or not parsed_url.netloc:
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Invalid URL format'
        }

    try:
        # Fetch the content of the specified URL
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context, timeout=10) as response:
            # Detect encoding from response headers
            encoding = response.headers.get_content_charset('utf-8')
            content = response.read().decode(encoding)

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description
        desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\'][^>]*name=["\']description["\']|'
                              r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
                              content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        if desc_match:
            description = (desc_match.group(1) or desc_match.group(2)).strip()
        else:
            description = "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except Exception as e:
        # If the page cannot be fetched or parsed, return default values
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': f'Unable to fetch page content: {str(e)}'
        }

import urllib.request
import ssl
import certifi
from html.parser import HTMLParser


class MetadataParser(HTMLParser):
    """HTML parser to extract title and meta description from HTML content."""

    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.title = None
        self.description = None
        self.in_title_tag = False

    def handle_starttag(self, tag, attrs):
        """Handle HTML start tags."""
        if tag.lower() == "title":
            self.in_title_tag = True
        elif tag.lower() == "meta":
            # Convert attributes list to dictionary for easier access
            attr_dict = dict(attrs)
            # Check if it's a description meta tag
            if (
                "name" in attr_dict
                and attr_dict["name"].lower() == "description"
                and "content" in attr_dict
            ):
                self.description = attr_dict["content"].strip()

    def handle_endtag(self, tag):
        """Handle HTML end tags."""
        if tag.lower() == "title":
            self.in_title_tag = False

    def handle_data(self, data):
        """Handle data content within tags."""
        if self.in_title_tag and self.title is None:
            self.title = data.strip()


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetch page metadata (title and description) from a given URL.

    Args:
        page_url: The URL of the webpage to fetch.

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing 'title' and 'description' keys.
        Values will be None if the corresponding metadata is not found.

    Raises:
        urllib.error.URLError: If there's an error fetching the URL.
        urllib.error.HTTPError: If the server returns an HTTP error.
        Exception: For other unexpected errors.
    """
    # Create SSL context with certifi CA certificates for secure HTTPS connections
    context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())

    try:
        # Fetch the webpage content
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, context=context) as response:
            # Read and decode HTML content (handle possible encoding issues)
            html_content = response.read().decode("utf-8", errors="ignore")

        # Parse metadata from HTML content
        parser = MetadataParser()
        parser.feed(html_content)

        # Return metadata as dictionary
        return {
            "title": parser.title,
            "description": parser.description
        }

    except Exception as e:
        raise e
